The program includes a required work integrated learning experience that allows students to apply classroom learning in real-world HR environments.

prerequisites.note: Admission is based on first applied, first qualified.
domestic: Minimum 65% in English Language Arts 30-2 or equivalent
Credit in Math 30-1 or Math 30-2 or equivalent
Successful completion of GED with minimum 520 in Language Arts: Reading and Writing
Satisfactory results on the Bow Valley College Admission and Placement Test
Completion of 30 credits from a recognized business program (departmental approval required)
international: Equivalent secondary education meeting English and Math requirements
Country-specific admission document requirements must be met

additionalInfo.transferNote: Transfer opportunities available to a variety of institutions.

name: Bachelor of Arts, General
description: The Bachelor of Arts, General at Athabasca University provides a broad liberal arts education in the humanities and social sciences. Students explore disciplines such as history, literature, philosophy, sociology, psychology, anthropology, and political studies while developing strong analytical, critical thinking, and communication skills. The program offers flexibility to tailor studies to personal interests and is delivered fully online.

prerequisites.note: Open admission. No formal academic requirements.
international: English language proficiency may be required

skills: Analysis of social and global issues

additionalInfo.transferNote: Transfer credit and Prior Learning Assessment (PLAR) available. Minimum 30 credits must be completed at AU.

name: Bachelor of Arts, Labour Studies Major
description: The Bachelor of Arts, Labour Studies Major at Athabasca University is an interdisciplinary online program that examines the role of labour and working people in society. Students explore labour history, work organization, women and unions, and the theory and practice of trade unions. Designed especially for trade union leaders and members, the program strengthens critical thinking and social analysis skills while preparing graduates to navigate complex workplace, policy, and labour relations environments.

prerequisites.note: Open admission. No formal academic requirements.
international: English language proficiency may be required

skills: Labour relations and collective bargaining analysis
Workplace policy and social justice advocacy

additionalInfo.transferNote: Transfer credit and Prior Learning Assessment (PLAR) available, including advanced credit for select union education programs. Minimum 30 credits must be completed at AU.

name: Bachelor of Arts, Political Economy Major
description: The Bachelor of Arts, Political Economy Major at Athabasca University examines the interconnected relationships between politics, society, and economics in a globalized world. Students explore global and Canadian political economy, public policy, economic systems, and the dynamics of a knowledge-based society and marketplace. This interdisciplinary online program develops analytical and critical thinking skills to help graduates understand complex political and economic challenges and prepare for diverse career paths.

prerequisites.note: Open admission. No formal academic requirements.
international: English language proficiency may be required

skills: Global and Canadian political economy research
Critical thinking and data-driven decision making

additionalInfo.transferNote: Transfer credit and Prior Learning Assessment (PLAR) available. Minimum 30 credits must be completed at AU.

name: Bachelor of Commerce, General
description: The Bachelor of Commerce, General at Athabasca University provides a comprehensive and technically focused business education covering marketing, finance, accounting, economics, information systems, strategic management, and organizational behaviour. Delivered fully online with monthly intakes, the program equips students with analytical, leadership, and problem-solving skills required for success in modern business environments. The Faculty of Business holds AACSB accreditation, placing it among the top business schools worldwide.
duration: 4 years (120 credits, self-paced online) or 2 years (post-diploma route)

prerequisites.note: Open admission. Applicants must be at least 16 years old. Applicants with an existing degree in business, commerce, administration, or management are not eligible.

name: Bachelor of Commerce, Finance Major
description: The Bachelor of Commerce, Finance Major at Athabasca University integrates finance and economics to provide a comprehensive understanding of financial markets, instruments, and investment decision-making. Students develop analytical skills to evaluate capital acquisition strategies, financial management practices, and economic influences on organizations and individuals. Delivered fully online with monthly start dates, this AACSB-accredited program prepares graduates for careers in financial services, corporate finance, and investment management.
duration: 4 years (120 credits, self-paced online) or 2 years (post-diploma route)

name: Bachelor of Human Resources and Labour Relations
description: The Bachelor of Human Resources and Labour Relations (BHRLR) at Athabasca University is a fully online, multidisciplinary degree examining employment relations within social, legal, political, and economic contexts. The program covers human resource management, labour relations, and human resource development, making it particularly valuable for trade unionists, managers, and HR professionals seeking leadership roles in workplace relations and organizational development.
credentials: Bachelor of Human Resources and Labour Relations (BHRLR)

prerequisites.note: Open admission. Applicants must be at least 16 years old.

name: Bachelor of Professional Arts, Criminal Justice Major
description: The Bachelor of Professional Arts (BPA), Criminal Justice Major at Athabasca University is a fully online diploma-to-degree program designed for working professionals and diploma graduates. The program provides a comprehensive understanding of criminal justice systems, policies, and institutional practices, covering areas such as community policing, youth justice, family violence, and criminal justice policy analysis. It combines prior professional experience with academic theory to prepare graduates for advanced roles or further graduate study.

Applicants may also qualify with a combination of post-secondary education and related work experience. name: Bachelor of Science, Architecture
description: The Bachelor of Science (BSc), Architecture at Athabasca University is a fully online program developed in collaboration with Architecture Canada and the Royal Architectural Institute of Canada (RAIC). The program integrates technical skills, architectural history and theory, professional practice, communication, and design studios with a strong emphasis on environmental and community sustainability. Students may pursue general study or the RAIC Syllabus route, preparing them for further professional study or careers in the built environment.

prerequisites.note: Open admission for general interest (minimum age 16). Additional requirements apply for the RAIC Syllabus route.

name: Bachelor of Science, Computing and Information Systems Major
description: The Bachelor of Science (BSc), Computing and Information Systems Major at Athabasca University is a fully online degree that integrates computer science and information systems. The program develops technical expertise in designing, developing, and managing computer-based systems while emphasizing strategic IT resource management. Students may follow a standard 4-year route or a post-diploma pathway with transfer credit options.
duration: 4 years (120 credits) or 2 years (post-diploma route, depending on transfer credit)

name: Post-LPN Bachelor of Nursing
description: The Post-LPN Bachelor of Nursing at Athabasca University is designed for Alberta Licensed Practical Nurses (LPNs) seeking to advance to a baccalaureate nursing degree. The program combines online theoretical coursework with required on-site clinical practice components and prepares graduates to write the NCLEX-RN exam. The curriculum aligns with the College of Registered Nurses of Alberta (CRNA) Entry-Level Competencies for Registered Nurses and supports pathways to graduate nursing education.
duration: 4 years (120 credits; blended delivery with clinical components)

prerequisites.note: Competitive admission. Applicants must be licensed Practical Nurses (LPNs) in Alberta and meet all program-specific requirements. Applications are not deferred and decisions are final.
international: Not applicable (program designed for Alberta LPNs)

additionalInfo.deliveryMode: Online with required in-person clinical practice
additionalInfo.transferNote: Admission limited to Alberta Licensed Practical Nurses. Clinical placements required.

name: University Certificate, Accounting
description: The University Certificate, Accounting at Athabasca University is a 30-credit online program designed to provide foundational knowledge in financial accounting, cost analysis, economics, commercial law, and statistics. The certificate can be completed in as little as one year and serves as a pathway to the Advanced Certificate in Accounting and the Bachelor of Commerce in Accounting. Many courses align with CPA prerequisite requirements.

prerequisites.note: Open admission. Applicants must be 16 years or older. Individuals with a prior undergraduate or graduate degree with a major in accounting are not eligible.
international: English language proficiency may be required

additionalInfo.transferNote: Minimum 15 credits must be completed at AU. Restrictions apply to senior-level business courses older than 10 years.
